creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
In 'general' IT world people consider either Block Devices or File Shares (NFS/SAMBA/...) and recently also Object Storage (S3).

The lsblk(8) on FreeBSD is just mine implementation of Linux lsblk(8) command idea - to list - well - block devices.

I agree with You that FreeBSD can have character devices instead of block devices when it comes to the specifics - but it does not matter to the outside world.

... and I would have to change the name from lsblk(8) to lsclk(8) :>

IMHO FreeBSD has a lot bigger problems like - for example - lack of ANY mention about VNET Jails (or networking related to it) in the FreeBSD Handbook or FAQ.

Many other FreeBSD technologies are not properly advertised or documented (Netgraph/...) - while they often do wonders and circles around Linux solutions ...

Regards,
vermaden
